司令官、お疲れ様です！<br>今日は、アリシアさんに借りていた本を、返しに来ただ:
Commander, thank you for your hard work today! I came to return a
book I borrowed from Alicia.
え？ どんな本を借りていたかって？<br>えーと……ただの国語辞典です。:
What? You want to know what book I borrowed? Well... it is just a
Japanese dictionary.
こないだおっしゃっていた『目の保養』の意味が理解出来なくて、<br>辞書には『美:
I didn't understand your 'feast for the eyes' comment, so I checked
the dictionary. It said it means 'beautiful things comfort the eyes'!
やはり、私の剣さばきが、それほどまでに美しいということですよね！<br>みなさん:
Just as I thought, it means my swordsmanship is that beautiful,
doesn't it? And it seems I'm useful to everyone, so I'm glad!
でも、ホントは周りの視線なんて、もうどうでもいいんですけどね。<br>司令官が私:
But honestly, I don't care about others' gazes anymore. If you,
Commander, look at me properly, that alone is enough for me...
